queries.query: What cooking technique is used to make tempura?
queries.answer: Tempura involves coating ingredients in a flour and egg batter and deep frying them very briefly to produce a light crispy coating over fresh ingredients.
queries.title: 12. Japan

queries.query: What is chicken teriyaki?
queries.answer: Chicken teriyaki is a technique that involves grilling food in a marinade of soy sauce, mirin, sake, onion and ginger.
queries.title: 12. Japan

queries.query: What is dashi traditionally made with?
queries.answer: Dashi is traditionally made with kombu, a strong-tasting kelp.
queries.title: 12. Japan

queries.query: Where did takoyaki originate?
queries.answer: Takoyaki originated in Osaka in the 1930s.
queries.title: 12. Japan

queries.query: What is inside a takoyaki?
queries.answer: A takoyaki is a ball of fried dough surrounding a tender piece of octopus.
queries.title: 12. Japan

queries.query: What is xiǎochī in Taiwanese cuisine?
queries.answer: Xiǎochī is a tapas-like tradition of serving a variety of small dishes, and is a famous aspect of Taiwanese cuisine.
queries.title: 22. Taiwan

queries.query: Where is the Taiwanese oyster omelette thought to have originated?
queries.answer: The oyster omelette is thought to have originated from the Teochew diaspora out of eastern Guangdong province of China.
queries.title: 22. Taiwan

queries.query: What gives the Taiwanese oyster omelette its thick consistency?
queries.answer: Small oysters are cooked with whisked eggs and sweet potato starch to create the thick consistency of the omelette.
queries.title: 22. Taiwan

queries.query: What principle do northern Chinese apply to food preparation?
queries.answer: Northern Chinese strongly encourage the concept of yin and yang in all aspects of food preparation, including colour, smell, flavour, and nutritional value.
queries.title: 65. Northern China

queries.query: Why is hot pot considered a healthy meal?
queries.answer: Hot pot is considered healthy because it is balanced, warming, and cooked without oil or frying.
queries.title: 65. Northern China

queries.query: What does 'yum cha' mean in Cantonese?
queries.answer: Yum cha means 'drink tea' in Cantonese, emphasising the importance of tea to the dim sum occasion.
queries.title: 65. Northern China

queries.query: What is a buuz typically filled with?
queries.answer: Buuz is a Mongolian dumpling most commonly filled with ground lamb.
queries.title: 75. Mongolia

queries.query: Why is rice not grown locally in Mongolia?
queries.answer: Rice does not grow well in Mongolia because the climate is too cold and dry, so it has a long history of importation from China.
queries.title: 75. Mongolia

queries.query: What type of rice is required to make onigiri?
queries.answer: Japanese-style short grain rice must be used for onigiri; if the rice is not sticky enough, the onigiri will not hold together.
queries.title: Cookbook:Onigiri

queries.query: What happens if spicy miso udon soup reaches a boil?
queries.answer: The delicate flavours of spicy miso udon do not hold up at high temperatures; if the soup reaches a boil it will be ruined.
queries.title: Cookbook:Spicy Miso Udon

queries.query: What is wonton filling essentially described as?
queries.answer: Wonton filling is essentially stir-fry, but with smaller particles and less cooking, placed hot into a wonton wrapper and cooked in broth.
queries.title: Cookbook:Wonton Soup

queries.query: What is a baozi?
queries.answer: A baozi is a type of steamed, filled bun in Chinese cuisine, similar to mantou but with a meat or vegetarian filling.
queries.title: Cookbook:Baozi (Steamed Yeasted Dumplings)

queries.query: At what meals can baozi be eaten?
queries.answer: Baozi can be eaten at any meal in Chinese culture, and is often eaten for breakfast.
queries.title: Cookbook:Baozi (Steamed Yeasted Dumplings)

queries.query: What is Red Bean Paste made from?
queries.answer: Red Bean Paste is a sweet paste made of azuki beans and sugar.
queries.title: Cookbook:Baozi (Steamed Yeasted Dumplings)

queries.query: What are Xiaolong Bao?
queries.answer: Xiaolong Bao are 'little dragon' dumplings, one of the most famous baozi fillings, usually soupy with spicy pork.
queries.title: Cookbook:Baozi (Steamed Yeasted Dumplings)

queries.query: Why should rice not be overwashed when making congee?
queries.answer: Overwashing the rice will wash out the starch, causing the congee not to congeal properly.
queries.title: Cookbook:Chinese Rice Porridge (Congee)

queries.query: What is fried corn described as a substitute for?
queries.answer: Fried corn is described as a good substitute for chips or fries.
queries.title: Cookbook:Fried Corn

queries.query: What type of vinegar is used in Kung Pao Chicken sauce?
queries.answer: Kung Pao Chicken sauce uses rice vinegar.
queries.title: Cookbook:Kung Pao Chicken

queries.query: What does the Chinese name for red bean paste (hóngdòushā) mean?
queries.answer: Hóngdòushā literally means 'red bean sand', referring to the paste's gritty texture.
queries.title: Cookbook:Red Bean Paste

queries.query: How long should wonton wrapper dough be refrigerated?
queries.answer: The wonton wrapper dough should be refrigerated for 30 minutes to an hour.
queries.title: Cookbook:Wonton Wrappers

queries.query: What is daigakuimo?
queries.answer: Daigakuimo is a dish of Japanese candied sweet potato.
queries.title: Cookbook:Daigakuimo (Japanese Candied Sweet Potato)

queries.query: What is jangjorim?
queries.answer: Jangjorim is a Korean dish consisting mainly of boiled or braised beef and soy sauce.
queries.title: Cookbook:Quail Egg and Chili Jangjorim

queries.query: What liquid ingredients are used to braise quail eggs in jangjorim?
queries.answer: The quail eggs are braised in water, soy sauce, sugar, refined rice wine, garlic, fresh ginger, and dried red chile peppers.
queries.title: Cookbook:Quail Egg and Chili Jangjorim

queries.query: What does Dalgona coffee refer to?
queries.answer: Dalgona coffee is a South Korean beverage made by whipping equal parts instant coffee, sugar and hot water into a foam, then placing it over milk.
queries.title: Cookbook:Dalgona Coffee

queries.query: What sauce is served alongside cream cheese wontons?
queries.answer: Cream cheese wontons are served with sweet and sour sauce.
queries.title: Cookbook:Cream Cheese Wontons

queries.query: What is the most common filling for onigiri?
queries.answer: The most common onigiri fillings are sour or salty ingredients, such as umeboshi (pickled plums) or tuna with mayonnaise, as they help preserve the rice.
queries.title: Cookbook:Onigiri

queries.query: What is the purpose of nori in onigiri?
queries.answer: Onigiri is frequently wrapped in nori to make it easier to eat.
queries.title: Cookbook:Onigiri

queries.query: What oil can be optionally added for flavor in broccoli stir fry?
queries.answer: Sesame oil can optionally be used when frying broccoli stir fry, in addition to regular frying oil.
queries.title: Cookbook:Broccoli Stir Fry

queries.query: What temperature is used to fry Cantonese crispy fried chicken?
queries.answer: The chicken is deep fried at 350°F (175°C), approximately 10 minutes per side until brown and cooked through.
queries.title: Cookbook:Cantonese Crispy Fried Chicken

queries.query: What is the oven temperature for Cantonese roast duck?
queries.answer: Cantonese roast duck is initially roasted at 450°F (220°C), then the temperature is reduced to 375°F (190°C) for the remainder of cooking.
queries.title: Cookbook:Cantonese Roast Duck

queries.query: How was meat preserved in ancient China?
queries.answer: Meat in ancient China was preserved with salt, vinegar, curing, and fermenting.
queries.title: Chinese cuisine

queries.query: What New World crops became staple foods in northern China?
queries.answer: Calorically dense potatoes and corn became staple foods across the northern Chinese plains after being introduced.
queries.title: Chinese cuisine

queries.query: How long ago did Chinese ancestors first cultivate rice and millet?
queries.answer: Chinese ancestors successfully planted millet, rice, and other grains about 8,000 to 9,000 years ago.
queries.title: Chinese cuisine

queries.query: What does baijiu literally mean?
queries.answer: Baijiu literally means 'white liquor'.
queries.title: Chinese cuisine

queries.query: What is siu yeh?
queries.answer: Siu yeh is a late-night fourth meal in Chinese culture, served from 9pm to 4am, similar to the Western concept of supper.
queries.title: Chinese cuisine

queries.query: What is chatang and what is it made from?
queries.answer: Chatang is Tianjin's traditional snack made of baked millet and glutinous millet flour, prepared by pouring boiling water into the mixed flour and adding sugar.
queries.title: Tianjin cuisine

queries.query: What cooking method characterises Wuxi-style cuisine?
queries.answer: Wuxi-style cuisine is characterised by the addition of sugar and soy sauce to many savoury dishes through hongshao (red braising), resulting in a fragrant, caramelised flavour.
queries.title: Jiangsu cuisine

queries.query: What UNESCO designation did Chengdu receive in 2011?
queries.answer: UNESCO declared Chengdu, the capital of Sichuan Province, a city of gastronomy in 2011.
queries.title: Sichuan cuisine

queries.query: What cuisines mainly influence Macanese cuisine?
queries.answer: Macanese cuisine is mainly influenced by Chinese cuisine, especially Cantonese cuisine, and European cuisine, predominantly Portuguese cuisine.
queries.title: Macanese cuisine

queries.query: What are the main traditional Japanese noodles?
queries.answer: Soba (thin buckwheat noodles) and udon (thick wheat noodles) are the main traditional Japanese noodles, while ramen is a modern import.
queries.title: Japanese cuisine

queries.query: What does the Japanese word for cooked rice (gohan or meshi) also mean?
queries.answer: The Japanese word for cooked rice, gohan or meshi, also means a 'meal', reflecting rice's fundamental importance in Japanese culture.
queries.title: Japanese cuisine

queries.query: What is ohaw in Ainu cuisine?
queries.answer: Ohaw is Ainu soup made from boiling fish or meats with various vegetables, and it is a central dish in Ainu food culture.
queries.title: Ainu cuisine

queries.query: What is gochujang?
queries.answer: Gochujang is a fermented bean paste made with red pepper powder.
queries.title: Korean cuisine

queries.query: What is bánh chưng?
queries.answer: Bánh chưng is sticky rice wrapped in banana leaves and stuffed with mung bean paste, lean pork, and black pepper, traditionally eaten during the Lunar New Year (Tết).
queries.title: Vietnamese cuisine

queries.query: What is Vietnamese sausage (giò) made from?
queries.answer: Vietnamese sausage (giò) is usually made from fresh ground pork and beef, wrapped in banana leaves and boiled.
queries.title: Vietnamese cuisine
